expectations.text: Recommends keeping yauth's OpenAPI spec separate from the app's spec
expectations.evidence: Title and line 3: 'Adding yauth does not require merging yauth's spec into yours. The recommended approach is to keep the two specs and clients separate.'

expectations.text: Shows how to use @yackey-labs/yauth-client alongside the app's orval-generated client
expectations.evidence: Lines 205-218: Side-by-side usage of listWidgets (orval) and createYAuthClient (yauth)

expectations.text: Explains that credentials: include on fetch requests automatically carries the session cookie
expectations.evidence: Lines 136-189: Full custom mutator with credentials: 'include' and orval config referencing it

expectations.text: Does NOT suggest merging yauth routes into the app's openapi.json
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'Your utoipa #[derive(OpenApi)] and all your #[utoipa::path(...)] annotations remain exactly as they are. yauth's routes simply do not appear in your spec'

expectations.text: Mentions that both clients coexist since yauth routes are under /api/auth
expectations.evidence: Lines 6-8: 'yauth routes are mounted under a dedicated prefix (/api/auth), while your app's routes live elsewhere. The two never overlap.'
